candidate_clusters_ranked.rejected_reason: topic too broad (mixed: negotiation, geopolitics, public-speaking, life-lessons, chess) — would not produce coherent Kasten
single-source (github only); cluster size below 5-6 partial threshold
size 4 below partial threshold; would need 3+ fresh zettels of which 2+ from new sources (yt, rd, web)
topic blend (substance neuroscience + sleep + spirituality + negotiation-psych) is too thematic-loose for a coherent eval Kasten — questions could not be reliably grounded

decision.rationale: Per plan Step 4: no organic cluster meets the strict gate (count>=7 AND distinct_sources>=3). The two clusters that hit the partial 5-6 threshold (lecture / psychology) are topic-mush and would not anchor a coherent eval. Falling back to the plan-specified default 'Knowledge Management & Personal Productivity' (slug 'knowledge-management'), which is orthogonal to AI/ML Foundations and intersects with several existing nodes (Pragmatic Engineer, Patrick Winston public speaking, Steve Jobs commencement, sleep/learning, programming workflow) — providing some warm-start grounding while requiring 5+ fresh zettels for source-type diversity.

fresh_zettels_needed.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — to be replaced with a real lecture/talk on PKM/Zettelkasten/note-taking workflows. Required source: youtube. Drop-in candidates: Niklas Luhmann zettelkasten talks, Andy Matuschak working-with-the-garage-door-up, Tiago Forte Building a Second Brain talks.
PLACEHOLDER — github repo for a popular note-taking / PKM tool (e.g., obsidian-zettelkasten plugin, logseq, or Anki) to provide a tooling perspective. Required source: github.
PLACEHOLDER — top reddit thread from r/Zettelkasten or r/PKMS on workflow disputes/tradeoffs to seed community-perspective answers. Required source: reddit.
PLACEHOLDER — generic-web essay on evergreen notes / spaced repetition (Andy Matuschak) for a long-form web article slot. Required source: web.
PLACEHOLDER — newsletter post on knowledge-work/AI-augmented PKM (Platformer or Pragmatic Engineer follow-up). Required source: newsletter — keeps newsletter rep balanced.

ui_ux_findings_during_audit.observation: Every node returned with source_type=undefined and title=null. Source-type is recoverable from id-prefix (yt-, gh-, rd-, ss-, web-, nl-, md-) but the API is dropping these fields server-side. Will degrade Phase 1 retrieval-side source-type boost (Task 9) and Phase 4 graph-score weighting (Task 24). Filed as pre-existing prod data-shape bug — not in current plan scope but referenced here for traceability.

Create Kasten button (id=create-kasten-btn, class=kastens-add-circle) is bound via getElementById at first paint. Modal opens correctly on FIRST click after page load (verified via screenshot ss_2381e8fpr — modal renders with Name/Quality/Zettels-to-include/Description/Cancel/Create). This matches Task 3's stale-handler pattern: handler works pre-async-auth-resettlement, fails after. Will be confirmed/repro'd by Task 3 implementer subagent.
Junk node 'web-test-title' (no tags, no title) appears in /api/graph response and inflates KASTENS-stats. Pre-existing test artifact — out of plan scope but flagged for cleanup.

next_actions_for_pipeline: Phase 0b/0c subagents proceed regardless of this artifact.
Eval Kasten construction (post-Task 26 deploy) will use slug 'knowledge-management' and add the 5 fresh zettels via Chrome MCP UI flow (not direct DB) so it co-validates Task 3's button-rebind fix.
Resolve F-1 (api/graph source_type/title null) before Task 28 eval computation — failing this means recency/source boosts cannot be measured.
